forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 54. West southwest wind 8 to 12 mph, with gusts as high as 18 mph.

forecast.name: Saturday
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly sunny. High near 64, with temperatures falling to around 62 in the afternoon. West southwest wind 7 to 14 mph, with gusts as high as 20 mph.

weather_report:
Tonight in Downtown San Francisco, a curtain of cloud drapes over the city, hinting at the cooler embrace of the sea breeze—the kind that envelops you in a gentle calm around the low of 54. The west-southwest winds whisper through the streets, measurable at 8 to 12 mph, carrying with them the distinct scent of the ocean mist. The darkness punctuated only by city lights creates a tranquil yet stirring landscape where the line between the sky and the bay blurs.

As the sun rises tomorrow, expect a slow unveiling. The forecast promises a mostly sunny day, with the high reaching around 64 before settling back into the comfortable embrace of 62 in the afternoon. Still, those gentle winds will persist, occasionally kicking up to 14 mph, ensuring that any lingering fog won’t overstay its welcome. With clarity on the horizon, consider dressing in layers—a light long-sleeve paired with a jacket for the morning chill would suit a day filled with leisurely exploration. Perhaps a stroll through one of the local markets or a quiet coffee on a sunlit patio would be well-received, allowing you to savor both the fleeting clarity and the cool air.
